rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings after subcomponents and connections are declared
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Align helper declaration types with the expression types to avoid type mismatches
Remove generated clauses that reference AADL properties directly unless they are exposed as explicit typed ports or constants
Omit range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Write output dependency contracts that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
